candidate_llm_application: Dual-channel low-frequency rumble broadcast for fleet drift coordination + ear-constriction channel prioritization

results.justification: Pure source-domain biology page on elephant communication. No LLM/fleet/drift content. Only matches the biological side-channel.
Cornell bioacoustic source-domain only. No connection to LLM fleet coordination.
Seismic localization of elephant rumbles - pure biology, source-domain only.
Agent Drift in MAS quantifies drift modes but does not propose dual-channel broadcast, periodic rumble vectors, nor channel prioritization. No content-word matches.
Turn-level adversarial intent drift detection within a session - not a fleet-replica broadcast mechanism. Different scope and mechanism.

ML monitoring literature review covers periodic sampling and probe sets but is a survey, not the specific dual-channel low-frequency rumble proposal with channel prioritization (ear-constriction analog). Functional similarity below 0.7 threshold; lacks the dual-channel switching mechanism and the long-period state-vector broadcast for soft adaptation.
Coordination architectural layer for LLM-MAS via info-controlled mechanisms / prediction markets - task-level fast coordination, not low-frequency periodic side-channel broadcast.

SAGS networks use a single LLM backbone to adapt across multi-channel networks; mentions channels but does not propose periodic state-vector rumble emission across replica fleet for drift coordination.

summary.delta_from_06_7: Disagrees with 06_7 on rank 6: 06_7 marked func=0.72 (hit); fresh verifier judges func=0.58 (no hit) because the review paper enumerates periodic-sampling/probe-set tools generally and lacks the specific dual-channel low-frequency state-broadcast + channel-prioritization design.
summary.notes: All eight results fail all three legs of the OR rule under fresh re-judgment.
